title: FreeBird Service Configuration
description: Configuration schema for `fb service` (Windows Service hosting mode). Describes the watch pipeline parameters and Windows Service registration metadata.

$schema.description: JSON Schema reference URL. Tolerated by the deserializer; used by IDEs for autocomplete.

watch.description: Watch pipeline configuration (mirrors `fb watch` CLI flags).

inputs.description: One or more input directories to scan for NetEase cache files (.uc / .uc!).

output.description: Output directory for decoded audio files.

integrity.description: Integrity check level. `auto` probes for `flac` at startup and falls back to `l1` if absent.

poll_interval.description: How often to poll input directories for new files. Duration string e.g. `10s`, `30s`, `2m`.

stability_checks.description: Number of consecutive stable size+mtime polls required before a file is considered ready for processing.

min_file_size.description: Minimum input file size in bytes before processing is attempted.

write_tags.description: Whether to write ID3/Vorbis/MP4 tags to decoded output files.

log_file.description: Path to the rolling log file. When omitted, defaults to a ProgramData path under the service identity.

bin.description: Absolute path to a pre-installed `flac` executable. Overrides PATH lookup and auto-install.

url.description: Override download URL for Windows auto-install of the official Xiph FLAC ZIP.

disable_auto_install.description: When true, suppresses the Windows auto-install attempt. Service will fall back to `l1` if `flac` is not on PATH.

api_rate_limit.description: NetEase API request rate limit in requests per second (Round 5 M-4). E.g. `0.5` allows one request every two seconds. Omit for no explicit rate limit.

name.description: Windows Service short name (used by `sc.exe` and the SCM).

display_name.description: Human-readable display name shown in services.msc.

description.default: Continuously decodes NetEase Cloud Music cache files into tagged MP3/FLAC/M4A using the FreeBird pipeline.

restart_policy.description: How the SCM should respond when the service process exits unexpectedly. `backoff` uses Windows' built-in exponential backoff via sc.exe failure actions.

log_to_event_log.description: Whether the service should also write a subset of events (start, stop, errors) to the Windows Event Log.
